title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table for express 5 routing, path syntax, parameters, and dispatch. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Express5 Path Syntax
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 3051 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Express5 Path Syntax.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Route Table Gate, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 3871 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Route Table Gate, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: If each intended endpoint succeeds once, routing is complete and conflict free.

stimulus.title: Route Table Gate failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: If each intended endpoint succeeds once, routing is complete and conflict free.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Route Table Gate condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Route Table Gate happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Table Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Route Table Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 8014 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Table Gate.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Route Table Gate, keep Route Table Gate connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.
